ky_cyclo_news_cases.long_name: Cumulative confirmed cyclosporiasis cases relayed by KY news media, by county/state
ky_cyclo_news_cases.short_description: Cumulative confirmed cyclosporiasis cases as reported by a trusted Kentucky news outlet relaying Kentucky Dept for Public Health (KDPH) figures.
ky_cyclo_news_cases.long_description: Cumulative confirmed cyclosporiasis case counts extracted from a trusted news article that relays Kentucky Dept for Public Health (KDPH) figures - NOT a direct KDPH dashboard/API feed. This is a lower-tier 'news' source (source_tier below) and must never be blended unlabeled with the dashboard-tier sources (fl_/in_/mi_/oh_/wv_/ca_/or_cyclo). Partial-county articles never contribute a zero for an unnamed county - absence here means 'not stated in this article', not 'zero cases'. An official KDPH source superseding a given as_of_date should retire the corresponding news row.
ky_cyclo_news_cases.statement: In {location}, {value} cumulative confirmed cyclosporiasis cases had been reported as of {time}, per news coverage.

ky_cyclo_news_cases_new.long_name: Newly reported confirmed cyclosporiasis cases since the prior news snapshot, by county/state
ky_cyclo_news_cases_new.short_description: Incident (newly reported) confirmed cyclosporiasis cases since the previous news-derived snapshot for the same geography.
ky_cyclo_news_cases_new.long_description: Derived measure: the difference in ky_cyclo_news_cases between two consecutive news-derived snapshots for the same geography. News coverage is event-driven, not scheduled, so the interval between snapshots is irregular and a 'new cases' value can span anywhere from days to months. The first snapshot for a geography has a missing (NA) value here, since there is no prior snapshot to difference against. A negative value would indicate a downward revision and is unexpected for a cumulative count - see raw/ky_cyclo_news_review.csv.gz, since check_monotonic() in reconcile.R routes cumulative decreases to review rather than silently landing them.
ky_cyclo_news_cases_new.statement: In {location}, {value} new confirmed cyclosporiasis cases were reported as of {time}, per news coverage.

ky_cyclo_news_reported.long_name: Cumulative confirmed+probable ('reported') cyclosporiasis cases relayed by KY news media, by county/state
ky_cyclo_news_reported.short_description: Cumulative case count as reported by a trusted KY news outlet when the article gives a single combined confirmed+probable figure rather than splitting them out.
ky_cyclo_news_reported.long_description: Some KDPH-sourced news articles give a single, larger 'reported' figure that combines confirmed and probable cyclosporiasis cases rather than stating them separately (news_extraction.schema.json's count_type = 'reported'). This column is that combined figure; it is not simply ky_cyclo_news_cases plus a probable column, since not every article reports probable cases at all. Compare against ky_cyclo_news_cases at the same as_of_date to see the residual attributable to probable/unconfirmed cases.
ky_cyclo_news_reported.statement: In {location}, {value} cumulative reported (confirmed+probable) cyclosporiasis cases had been reported as of {time}, per news coverage.

ky_cyclo_news_hospitalized.long_name: Cumulative cyclosporiasis-associated hospitalizations relayed by KY news media, by county/state
ky_cyclo_news_hospitalized.short_description: Cumulative cyclosporiasis-associated hospitalizations as reported by a trusted KY news outlet relaying KDPH figures.
ky_cyclo_news_hospitalized.long_description: Cumulative count of cyclosporiasis-associated hospitalizations extracted from a trusted news article relaying KDPH figures. Reported far less consistently than case counts - most articles do not state a hospitalization figure at all, so this column will be sparse/missing for many (geography, time) rows rather than zero-filled.
ky_cyclo_news_hospitalized.statement: In {location}, {value} cumulative cyclosporiasis-associated hospitalizations had been reported as of {time}, per news coverage.

ky_news_cyclosporiasis_2026.name: Kentucky cyclosporiasis case counts, as relayed by trusted news media
ky_news_cyclosporiasis_2026.organization: Kentucky Department for Public Health (KDPH), via allowlisted news outlets
ky_news_cyclosporiasis_2026.description: During the 2026 Kentucky cyclosporiasis outbreak, KDPH's own county-level case data was not available through a public dashboard/API, so this source instead harvests trusted news articles (Google News RSS discovery, restricted to an allowlist of KY public media and major dailies/network affiliates) that relay KDPH-attributed figures, extracts structured facts via an LLM against a fixed JSON schema, and reconciles them (FIPS resolution, Sum(county) vs state-total residual check, cumulative-monotonicity check vs prior snapshots). This is explicitly a LOWER-TRUST tier than the dashboard scrapers in this project (fl_/in_/mi_/oh_/wv_/ca_/or_cyclo) and must never be blended with them unlabeled. Article prose is never stored - only the extracted county/state -> count facts, the source URL, and attribution.
ky_news_cyclosporiasis_2026.restrictions: Underlying figures are Kentucky government (KDPH) public-health data relayed through copyrighted news reporting; only the facts (geography, count, date) are stored, not article text, per fair-use/facts-are-not-copyrightable practice.
